results.title: Singapore bank DBS keeps 2025 outlook, UOB trims expectations after mixed Q2 results - Reuters
results.content: SINGAPORE, Aug 7 (Reuters) - Singaporean banks posted mixed second-quarter earnings on Thursday, with DBS Group (DBSM.SI), opens new tab maintaining its 2025 outlook and United Overseas Bank (UOBH.SI), opens new tab resuming guidance but trimming expectations amid macroeconomic uncertainty. The banks' results followed that of peer Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp (OCBC.SI), opens new tab which posted on Friday an in-line second-quarter net profit, but cut its 2025 net interest income expectations and flagged persisting tariff uncertainty. results.title: Singapore bank UOB cites 'softer operating environment' as net profit falls 4% on-year - Reuters
results.content: # Singapore bank UOB cites 'softer operating environment' as net profit falls 4% on-year | Reuters. ## Browse World. ## Browse Business. # Singapore bank UOB cites 'softer operating environment' as net profit falls 4% on-year. Image 2: A view of the United Overseas Bank (UOB) signage in Singapore. A view of the United Overseas Bank (UOB) signage in Singapore May 3, 2023. SINGAPORE, May 7 (Reuters) - Singapore's ‌United Overseas Bank, or UOB (UOBH.SI), opens new tab, reported on Thursday a 4% annual fall in first-quarter net profit that, despite beating expectations, reflected the impact a precarious market backdrop and rate headwinds have ​had on the lender. The Reuters Iran Briefing newsletter keeps you informed with the latest developments and analysis of the Iran war. For the quarter, the bank's net profit declined ​to S$1.44 billion ($1.14 billion) from S$1.49 billion a year earlier, though it beat the mean estimate ‌of nearly ⁠S$1.38 billion from three analysts polled by LSEG. *   About Reuters, opens new tab.

results.title: UOB moves tech workforce to fuel Southeast Asia digital growth - Singapore Business Review
results.content: United Overseas Bank (UOB) has begun relocating its technology and innovation workforce to the Punggol Digital District, marking the start of its move into Singapore’s first smart and sustainable business district. The bank started operations at its $500m global technology and innovation centre on 4 May with nearly 400 employees. It plans to base more than 3,000 employees, mainly in technology, digital, and innovation roles, at the facility by October. UOB is the first bank to establish a presence in the district, according to a press release. It added that the move supports its digital-first strategy and Singapore’s Smart Nation agenda, and enables collaboration and innovation across its regional operations. Lawrence Goh, Head of Group Technology and Operations at UOB, said the relocation places the bank’s teams within Singapore’s innovation ecosystem and supports the development of digital banking systems across ASEAN. UOB said it is expanding its use of AI across operations. It also operates the UOB-IMDA AIDA Centre of Excellence to train early-career data and AI professionals.

results.title: Singapore banks show weak brand-experience alignment in 2025 - Asian Business Review
results.content: Banks in Singapore showed the weakest alignment between their brand promises and customer experiences, according to Forrester’s 2025 Total Experience Score rankings for financial services firms. Of the six banks assessed — Citibank, DBS, HSBC, OCBC, Standard Chartered, and United Overseas Bank — most struggled to match how they present their brands with what customers actually experience. results.title: UOB: Digital Tools Enable Green Transition in Southeast Asia - FinTech Magazine
results.content: United Overseas Bank's sustainability report reveals how fintech and digital banking are enabling a rapid green transition across the Southeast Asia region. United Overseas Bank (UOB) has mobilised US$70.1bn in sustainable finance in 2025, demonstrating how financial institutions are leveraging technology platforms to support the transition to green revenue across Southeast Asia. UOB's purpose is to build the future of ASEAN, supported by its sustainability framework and underpinned by digital banking technology. To help tackle climate change, UOB is committed to building the resilience of its lending and investment portfolio, while supporting the region's transition to a low-carbon economy through technology-enabled financial services. It worked on enhancing its sustainable finance frameworks to deepen its support of SMEs' decarbonisation efforts, with technology enabling more granular assessment of environmental impact. UOB's sustainable financing portfolio covers a variety of sectors, including data centres, renewable energy and healthcare, with digital platforms facilitating investment across these diverse areas.

results.title: UOL Sells 99% Of Residential Project At Launch Weekend Amid Resilient Singapore Demand - Forbes
results.content: # UOL Sells 99% Of Residential Project At Launch Weekend Amid Resilient Singapore Demand UOL Group—controlled by the family of late banking tycoon Wee Cho Yaw—and partner CapitaLand Development have sold nearly all the units at their residential project during the weekend launch, adding to signs of resilient demand in Singapore even after the government’s property curbs. UOL’s Upperhouse at Orchard Boulevard was among the top sellers, with about half of the 301-unit residential skyscraper sold during the launch weekend. UOL, together with its unit Singapore Land and Kheng Leong Holdings—the Wee family’s privately held property developer—bid against eight other groups, including Malaysian billionaire Quek Leng Chan’s GuocoLand, for the 10,399 square meter site on Dorset Road, near the Singapore central business district.

results.title: American Airlines CEO Rejects United Merger, Hints At Alaska Deal - Live and Let's Fly
results.content: Home » American Airlines » American Airlines CEO Rejects United Merger, Hints At Alaska Deal. # American Airlines CEO Rejects United Merger, Hints At Alaska Deal. American Airlines CEO Robert Isom reportedly told employees that a merger with United Airlines is off the table, but signaled that other transactions could make sense, including a closer partnership with Alaska Airlines or the acquisition of select Spirit Airlines assets. While Isom ruled out a merger with United, he did not rule out a closer partnership with Alaska:. But his openness to Alaska or selective Spirit assets shows American is at least thinking creatively about how to close the gap with stronger rivals. AA may not be in a position to move right now in the way United (or Delta) is, but we will see how closely the partnership with Alaska Airlines becomes in the weeks and months ahead. * ## Malarkey: United CEO Scott Kirby Claims Buying American Airlines Would Create Jobs, Lower Fares, And Save America.

results.title: Medialink Group Launches Japan Subsidiary - Variety
results.content: Variety # Hong Kong-Based IP Management Company Medialink Group Expands Into Japan With New Subsidiary Launch (EXCLUSIVE) Medialink Group Limited has officially launched a new subsidiary in Japan, marking a significant expansion for the Hong Kong-based intellectual property management company. The new Japanese subsidiary will focus on three key areas to expand Medialink’s IP management ecosystem: pursuing merger and acquisition opportunities or strategic investments in IP-related companies in Japan, co-investing in Japanese intellectual properties, and forming strategic partnerships with Japanese IP owners and licensors.
